description: Create the Phase 0 baseline document shell for the current Windows Electron Dynamic Island behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-phase0-baseline.md with top-level headings for Scope, Runtime Environment, Evidence Index, and Phase 0 Acceptance.
Add a one-sentence scope note that the document records current Windows behavior before mac migration implementation.
Check that every heading is unique and the file opens correctly from repository root.

description: Create the state specification document shell for review, todo, and visual state behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with headings for Startup/Auth, Review Rules, Todo Rules, Visual State Machine, and Reminder Trigger Rules.
Add an empty table template under each heading with columns: Condition, Trigger, Expected UI, Evidence.
Check markdown structure consistency and confirm heading order matches the Phase 0 scope.

description: Create the interaction specification document shell for hover, click-through, pointer, and gesture behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-interaction-spec.md with headings for Hover Activation, Click-Through Toggle, Pointer Gestures, and Trackpad Gestures.
Add a reusable behavior row template with fields for trigger threshold, debounce, and recovery behavior.
Check that the document contains no behavior claims yet and only shell content.

description: Create the animation specification document shell for transition timing and motion sequencing.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-animation-spec.md with headings for Expand/Collapse, Mode Switch, Hover Motion, and Reminder-Triggered Motion.
Add an animation table template with fields for duration, easing, trigger, and observed result.
Check that all headings and table columns are present and readable in markdown preview.

description: Create the music takeover specification document shell for playback-driven island behavior.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-music-spec.md with headings for Providers/Inputs, Mode Switching, Playback States, and Degraded Fallback.
Add a behavior matrix template with fields for media event, widget state change, and user-visible output.
Check that the shell is documentation-only and contains no unverified runtime conclusions.

description: Create the Phase 0 migration checklist shell used to gate the move into Phase 1.
steps: Create docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md with sections for State, Interaction, Animation, Reminder, and Music coverage.
Add checklist rows with fields: Item, Expected Behavior, Evidence Link, Status.
Check that checklist status defaults are unset and ready for later fill tasks.

description: Add startup and authentication behavior details to the state spec from current widget code and visible runtime behavior.
steps: Trace startup and authentication-related logic in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and front-end/electron/main.cjs.
Use browser MCP to run the visible startup path and capture one authenticated and one unauthenticated observation.
Document startup/auth condition-to-UI mapping in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md under Startup/Auth.
Check that each documented rule includes at least one concrete code or runtime evidence reference.

description: Add Review Rules rows from static code paths without runtime sampling.
steps: Trace review-related data and rendering branches in front-end/src/components/DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Review Rules in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with condition, trigger, expected UI, and code evidence columns.
Check that placeholder rows are removed and every new row includes at least one concrete code location.

description: Add Todo Rules endpoint and rendering rows from existing frontend and API code.
steps: Trace todo-related request and rendering paths in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and related API call sites.
Fill Todo Rules in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md for /todos/stats, /todos/tasks, and /todos/tasks/:id/status with expected UI impact.
Check that each row contains endpoint, trigger, expected UI, and a direct code evidence reference.

description: Add Todo status-update persistence assumptions from code contracts and local schema references.
steps: Trace todo status update call flow in front-end code and backend controller/service path where available.
Add a concise contract note in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md describing write intent and expected persisted state effect.
Check the note references concrete code paths and avoids unverified runtime claims.

description: Add the Visual State Machine transition matrix from component state logic.
steps: Trace collapsed, activity, and expanded transition triggers in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Visual State Machine rows in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with source condition, trigger, expected state, and evidence.
Check that one explicit row states reminder auto-open is a trigger path instead of a standalone mode.

description: Add Reminder Trigger Rules from reminderTime and guard refs in widget code.
steps: Trace reminder-related refs, key guards, and auto-open conditions in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Reminder Trigger Rules in docs/mac-island-state-spec.md with due condition, trigger, recovery expectation, and evidence.
Check that the section distinguishes time trigger logic from display mode definitions.

description: Add Hover Activation and Click-Through Toggle rules from renderer and Electron bridge code.
steps: Trace hover handling and ignore mouse event toggling in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and front-end/electron/main.cjs.
Fill Hover Activation and Click-Through Toggle sections in docs/mac-island-interaction-spec.md.
Check each row includes trigger threshold or guard, recovery behavior, and evidence link.

description: Add Pointer and Trackpad gesture rules from gesture accumulators and cooldown logic.
steps: Trace gesture delta accumulators, lock flags, and cooldown timers in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Pointer Gestures and Trackpad Gestures sections in docs/mac-island-interaction-spec.md with action mapping.
Check that all template rows are replaced and each rule includes code evidence.

description: Add Expand/Collapse and Mode Switch animation rows from current UI animation definitions.
steps: Trace expand/collapse and mode-switch animation configuration in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx and related style hooks.
Fill corresponding sections in docs/mac-island-animation-spec.md with duration/easing/trigger/observed-result fields.
Check rows contain concrete code references and no placeholder entries remain in those two sections.

description: Add Hover Motion and Reminder-Triggered Motion animation rows from existing implementation.
steps: Trace hover-related motion and reminder-triggered open/collapse animation paths in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx.
Fill Hover Motion and Reminder-Triggered Motion sections in docs/mac-island-animation-spec.md.
Check that each row links to a specific code location and avoids inferred timing without source evidence.

description: Add music behavior rows from MusicService, MusicWorker, and renderer IPC listeners.
steps: Trace music event payload flow across front-end/electron/MusicService.cjs, MusicWorker.cjs, and DynamicIslandWidget.tsx listeners.
Fill Providers/Inputs, Mode Switching, Playback States, and Degraded Fallback in docs/mac-island-music-spec.md.
Check each row includes event trigger, state effect, user-visible result, and code evidence.

description: Populate the migration checklist with concrete evidence links from all Phase 0 spec documents.
steps: Replace template rows in docs/mac-island-migration-checklist.md with concrete checklist items for state, interaction, animation, reminder, and music.
Link each checklist item to at least one specific section row in the spec documents.
Check that every checklist item has a clear pass condition and no empty status fields for prepared rows.

description: Add a Phase 1 entry gate note after checking Phase 0 checklist coverage.
steps: Compare completed checklist categories with Phase 0 outputs and focus points in 灵动岛迁移方案.md.
Write a concise go/no-go note in docs/mac-island-phase0-baseline.md under Phase 0 Acceptance.
Check that the gate note lists explicit completion criteria and references checklist sections.

description: Create the mac-island project skeleton directory and Xcode project container for a standalone macOS app.
steps: Create mac-island/ with MemoryFlowIsland.xcodeproj and MemoryFlowIsland target group structure.
Create initial folder layout: App, Window, MenuBar, Preferences, UI, Resources under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/.
Check that project and group paths are consistent with the Phase 1 structure plan in 灵动岛迁移方案.md.

description: Add app bootstrap entry files for the native app startup path.
steps: Add MemoryFlowIslandApp.swift, AppDelegate.swift, and SceneCoordinator.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/App/.
Wire app launch flow so SceneCoordinator can initialize menu bar and window controllers.
Check that bootstrap files compile in target membership and have no unresolved symbols.

description: Create native island window shell classes with transparent floating window behavior.
steps: Add IslandPanel.swift and IslandWindowController.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Window/.
Configure a transparent, borderless, always-on-top style suitable for island shell behavior.
Check that window shell classes can instantiate and expose show/hide entry methods without business logic coupling.

description: Add notch-position helper placeholders for Phase 1 geometry readiness.
steps: Add NotchLayoutEngine.swift and DisplayObserver.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Window/.
Implement minimal API surfaces for screen bounds input and top-center placement output.
Check that IslandWindowController can reference the helper APIs without compile errors.

description: Add SwiftUI island placeholder view and connect it to the native window host.
steps: Create UI/IslandRootView.swift with a simple placeholder island appearance and static text marker.
Embed IslandRootView in the native window content pipeline from IslandWindowController.
Check that app launch can render the placeholder island content inside the native window shell.

description: Add menu bar controller and base menu entries for the native app shell.
steps: Create StatusBarController.swift and StatusMenuBuilder.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/MenuBar/.
Add base menu entries: Show/Hide Island, Preferences, and Quit with stub action handlers.
Check that menu bar icon appears and menu actions are callable without app crash.

description: Add empty preferences window skeleton and wire menu action entry.
steps: Create PreferencesWindowController.swift and PreferencesView.swift under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Preferences/.
Wire the Preferences menu item to open the preferences window controller.
Check that preferences window can be opened repeatedly without duplicate controller lifecycle issues.

description: Add app resource placeholders and basic mac app metadata for standalone launch.
steps: Create Resources/Info.plist and Assets.xcassets placeholders under mac-island/MemoryFlowIsland/Resources/.
Set minimal bundle metadata required for app launch and menu bar presentation.
Check that project build settings point to the new resource files correctly.

description: Add a short Phase 1 scaffold handoff note describing generated native app modules.
steps: Create or update a Phase 1 note under docs/ describing App, Window, MenuBar, Preferences, UI, and Resources modules.
Document explicit non-goals for this phase: no review/todo feature migration, no auth/API integration, no music provider migration.
Check that file paths in the handoff note match actual project skeleton paths.

description: Append Phase 1 acceptance-gate checklist items for native shell readiness.
steps: Add checklist items for: app can launch, menu bar icon is visible, and a basic top island window can be shown.
Place the checklist in an existing migration tracking doc used by this repository.
Check that each checklist item has a clear pass condition and is scoped only to Phase 1 skeleton goals.

description: Expose reusable screen metrics for native island positioning.
steps: Add a Window-layer screen metrics model that carries frame, visible frame, safe-area insets, backing scale, and a stable display identity.
Update DisplayObserver to return the screen metrics model instead of only a raw frame.
Check IslandWindowController can request the metrics model without parsing NSScreen directly.

description: Detect whether the target display needs notch-aware placement.
steps: Add a helper in the Window layer that marks a screen as notch-bearing or flat-top from the collected metrics.
Keep the decision isolated from IslandWindowController so later phases can reuse the same display classification.
Check both notch and non-notch sample inputs compile through the placement path.

description: Center the island shell against the notch-safe top region on notch displays.
steps: Update NotchLayoutEngine to compute the island origin from the top safe region instead of raw visible-frame midpoint placement.
Keep Phase 2 top-margin constants explicit inside the layout engine.
Check the engine returns a centered frame for a notch-display metrics fixture.

description: Place the island shell in a stable top-center fallback on non-notch displays.
steps: Add a separate fallback calculation for flat-top displays that keeps the entire shell inside the visible area.
Route the non-notch path through the same layout result type used by notch displays.
Check the fallback frame stays fully visible for a flat-top metrics fixture.

description: Show the island on the correct display using the new placement result.
steps: Update IslandWindowController show-path logic to request screen metrics and apply the layout-engine result before ordering the panel front.
Store the last applied display identity and frame in the controller for later re-anchor work.
Check repeated show calls reuse the new placement path without duplicate setup.

description: Keep the island on the same display when screen parameters change.
steps: Extend the Window/display layer to resolve the preferred screen by the last applied display identity when that screen still exists.
Fall back to the current main screen only when the previous target screen disappears.
Check a screen reorder or display attach-or-detach event can still produce one valid target display.

description: Re-anchor the island after display arrangement or resolution changes.
steps: Extend DisplayObserver callback wiring so IslandWindowController receives a single reposition signal for screen-parameter changes.
Reapply the latest layout result when the display configuration changes.
Check the observer path still compiles and triggers one reposition entry point.

description: Re-anchor the island after the Mac wakes from sleep.
steps: Register workspace wake notifications in DisplayObserver and map them to the same reposition callback used by display changes.
Keep observer cleanup symmetrical for app shutdown and controller deinit.
Check wake observation can be started and stopped without leaked observers.

description: Keep the island panel floating above normal windows without stealing focus.
steps: Refine IslandPanel style, collection behavior, and activation flags for a nonactivating, all-spaces, fullscreen-safe shell.
Keep the panel compatible with later hover interaction work.
Check showing the island does not require making the app the main active window.

description: Add shell size presets for collapsed and expanded placeholder frames.
steps: Define explicit shell size constants or presets in the Window layer for compact and expanded placeholder states.
Expose a controller path that can switch between the presets without introducing Phase 3 business state.
Check the window frame changes compile cleanly and remain centered after a preset switch.

description: Prevent the expanded shell shadow from being cropped by the panel frame.
steps: Adjust panel content hosting, padding, or shadow margins so the larger shell can render without clipping rounded edges or shadow.
Keep the visual fix inside the native shell layer rather than future business views.
Check the expanded preset keeps its visible bounds and shadow inside the panel frame.

description: Expose a native click-through toggle for the island panel.
steps: Add a controller or panel API that switches ignoresMouseEvents on and off without recreating the window.
Keep the API callable from later hover coordination code.
Check the toggle can flip in both directions while the panel remains visible.

description: Add pointer-entry detection that works while the island is in click-through mode.
steps: Introduce a small hover monitor in the native shell layer that can detect pointer entry into the island hotspot even when the panel is not accepting mouse events.
Send hover-start callbacks into IslandWindowController instead of mutating UI state directly.
Check the hover monitor lifecycle starts and stops with the window controller.

description: Switch the island into interactive hover mode when the pointer enters the hotspot.
steps: Wire hover-start events to disable click-through and keep the panel interactive while the pointer is over the island.
Keep the hover activation path isolated from later Phase 6 gesture logic.
Check entering the hotspot leads to the interactive window path without reopening the panel.

description: Return the island to click-through mode after hover exit.
steps: Add hover-exit handling and a small recovery rule that re-enables click-through after the pointer leaves the island.
Keep the recovery path in IslandWindowController so future gesture code can share it.
Check repeated enter-and-exit cycles do not leave the panel stuck in interactive mode.

description: Append Phase 2 acceptance items for window positioning and recovery behavior.
steps: Add checklist items for notch placement, non-notch fallback, display attach-or-detach recovery, resolution-change recovery, wake recovery, click-through, and hover activation in the existing migration tracking doc.
Keep each checklist item scoped to one observable window behavior with a clear pass condition.
Check the new checklist items cover only Phase 2 shell behavior and not later business-state work.

description: Add a short Phase 2 window-system handoff note for the next implementation slice.
steps: Create or update a Phase 2 note under docs/ that names the active Window-layer files and the expected acceptance path.
Record explicit non-goals: no review-or-todo data migration, no auth flow, no music takeover, and no Phase 6 gesture polish in this slice.
Check the note points only to files and behaviors that belong to the Phase 2 shell.
